description_long: This object consists of four square signs, each featuring a different accessibility symbol commonly found in urban environments. The first sign displays the International Symbol of Access, a blue wheelchair user on a white background with a blue border. The second sign shows an ear with a diagonal line, representing hearing assistance, in blue and white. The third sign features a pair of eyeglasses, indicating visual assistance, on a gray background with a blue border. The fourth sign again shows the International Symbol of Access, but with a green background and black border. All signs are flat, thin, and appear to be made of metal or plastic with printed graphics. The backs of the signs are plain and gray, and the sides are extremely thin, indicating a lightweight construction.
description: A set of four flat, square accessibility signs featuring wheelchair, hearing, and visual assistance symbols in blue, white, gray, and green.
description_view_0: Four square signs with accessibility symbols are visible from the front, showing detailed graphics and color differences.
description_view_1: The signs are viewed from the side, appearing as thin vertical lines with no visible graphics.
description_view_2: The backs of the four signs are visible, all plain gray with no symbols or markings.
description_view_3: Another side view, showing the thin profile of the signs as vertical lines.
